---
title: "Scenario Test: {Description}"
date: YYYY-MM-DD
categories: [scenario]
status: pass          # pass | fail | partial-pass | in-progress
branch: "main"        # or feature branch being tested
---

<!--
TEMPLATE — copy to testing-log/{date}-{description}.qmd.
Use for targeted tests of a specific edge case or pipeline configuration not covered
by the standard integration test profiles (e.g., a particular genetic architecture,
a new three-species combination, or a scenario-specific parameter setting).
See docs/testing-workflow.qmd for guidance.
-->

One paragraph describing what scenario is being tested and why it warrants a standalone entry
(i.e., what makes this edge case interesting or risky).

---

## Summary Checklist

Fill in the **Result** column as you work through each step.

| # | Step | Environment | Pass criterion | Result |
|---|------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| 1 | Pipeline run | local / HPC | exits 0, all tasks succeed | [ ] |
| 2 | Integration tests | local | all tests pass | [ ] |
| 3 | Specific assertions | local R session | scenario-specific checks pass | [ ] |

---

## Scenario Description

| Aspect | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Profile used | `test` / `test_cv_pool` / custom |
| Key parameters | — |
| Why this scenario matters | — |
| How it differs from standard integration tests | — |

---

## Setup

Any configuration or data generation steps needed before the pipeline run.

```bash
#| eval: false
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
# Setup commands here
```

---

## Pipeline Run

```bash
#| eval: false
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile <profile>,docker \
  --<param> <value> \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-scenario
```

Pipeline output:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

---

## Test Results

```bash
#| eval: false
TEST_DB_DIR=<path>/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=<path>/work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=<path>/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result: <!-- PASS / FAIL -->
```
# Paste test runner output here
```

---

## Specific Assertions

Any checks beyond the standard test suite — R snippets, manual inspections, or ad-hoc validations specific to this scenario.

```r
#| eval: false
# Example: inspect a specific output file or value
library(arrow)
db_dir <- "<path>/db"
# ...
```

---

## Notes

<!-- Findings, caveats, or follow-up items. -->
